name: What Cass Is Buying
description: Factor Loy has been moving unusual black stones through the Greasewater market for eight months. Merchant Prince Cass pays above market rate and never asks about sourcing. Loy wants to know what he's been carrying.
accept_text: He produces a small box from inside his coat with the careful movement of someone who has been carrying something they wanted to identify for several months. Inside: a fragment, dark, smaller than a finger joint. 'Eight months ago, new cargo category appeared in the Greasewater flow.' He sets the box on the table. 'Multiple sources, all from the Wastes interior, all brought out through salvagers who were very specifically not curious about what they'd found.' He looks at the fragment. 'Cass — my principal — placed a standing order. Any quantity, priority routing, above-market purchase price, no questions about origin.' He closes the box. 'I've been moving this material for eight months. I don't know what it is.' He slides the box toward you. 'The instruments I have access to don't produce a reading that makes sense. The fragment doesn't register as Aetherium and doesn't register as anything else I have a category for.' He looks at you. 'There's a researcher who's been working the black vault below the Wastes. I have it from a merchant who passed through two months ago.' He taps the box. 'Take the fragment. Find the researcher. Tell me what it is.' He pauses. 'Tell me also if there's any reason Cass should not be buying it.'
complete_text: Pell looks at the fragment for six seconds without speaking, which is longer than she usually looks at anything without commenting on it. Then: 'Where did this come from?' Her voice has the quality of a scientist who has found a data point that requires revision of her framework. She opens her own case and puts the fragment next to the disc she's been carrying for six years. The resonance signatures, even at the fragment's size, are clearly related. 'This is the same material.' She says it to herself. Then to you: 'Merchant Prince Cass has a standing order on this.' She sets down her instrument. 'The Ministry excluded this material from the official survey sixty years ago. Someone placed the restriction. Someone in the Ministry research division decided this material should stay out of general circulation.' She pauses. 'Cass is operating outside official channels by definition — that's what the Merchant Princes do. But she has a standing order on material that the Ministry doesn't want studied, eight months after the Grid reactivated, from sources in the Wastes interior.' She looks at the fragment. 'Tell Loy: it's pre-Grid crystal, possibly storage media, responds to the Grid's reactivation signal. Tell him it's not dangerous to handle. Tell him also: someone else already knows what it is, knew sixty years ago, and decided no one else should know.' She closes the case carefully. 'Tell him I want to know who Cass bought it for.'
